customers.name: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider
customers.description: A rider using the marketplace for commuting, airport trips, nights out, and everyday city travel. Success means getting a reliable ride quickly at a price that feels fair, with clear pickup, ETA, and safety signals throughout the trip.
careAbout: Short waiting time at busy hours
Easy airport, reserve, and commute use cases
Fast issue resolution if the trip fails
winsThem: Fast request-to-match flow with clear pickup guidance
Stable pricing and visible product options such as Reserve and Share
Consistent trip tracking and safety toolkit
Accurate airport and destination flows
Responsive support when a trip goes wrong
theirFear: Paying surge prices without understanding why
Missed pickups, long waits, or repeated driver cancellations
Feeling unsafe or unsupported during a trip

jobsToBeDone.name: Book a reliable ride for a time-sensitive trip
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Turn a need to get somewhere into a completed ride with predictable pickup, travel time, and payment.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: A completed trip that feels fast, safe, and worth the price paid.

steps.step: Define the trip and compare options
steps.description: Choose pickup, destination, trip timing, and whether standard, premium, reserve, share, or airport-specific options best fit the moment.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Trip Request and Intent Capture
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Captures pickup, destination, party size, timing, and rider preferences.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Dynamic Pricing and Incentives
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides upfront fares, surge logic, and product-level tradeoffs such as price versus wait time.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Reserve, Share and Airport Orchestration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Packages special flows for scheduled rides, pooled trips, and airport pickup or drop-off.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider JTBD step 1: Define the trip and compare options
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Book a reliable ride for a time-sensitive trip": Define the trip and compare options.

steps.step: Get matched and navigate pickup
steps.description: Confirm who is arriving, whether pickup is still on plan, and where the best meeting point is.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Dispatch and Matching Engine
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Finds the best available driver and manages rematch or cancellation logic.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: ETA, Maps and Routing Intelligence
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Guides both rider and driver to a workable pickup and provides live ETA updates.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Safety and Trust Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Surfaces identity, trip sharing, RideCheck, PIN verification, and incident help.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider JTBD step 2: Get matched and navigate pickup
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Book a reliable ride for a time-sensitive trip": Get matched and navigate pickup.

steps.step: Track, complete, and settle the trip
steps.description: Stay informed during the ride, arrive where expected, and pay or resolve issues without friction.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Rider Identity, Wallet and Payments
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports profile, family, payment methods, receipts, and refund flows.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Safety and Trust Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Handles support, reporting, and remediation if the trip deviates or fails.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Feeds quality scoring, trip-quality measurement, and operational follow-up.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider JTBD step 3: Track, complete, and settle the trip
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Book a reliable ride for a time-sensitive trip": Track, complete, and settle the trip.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider JTBD infographic: Book a reliable ride for a time-sensitive trip
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for the job "Book a reliable ride for a time-sensitive trip" for customer "Urban Time-Sensitive Rider".

jobsToBeDone.name: Use ride sharing confidently in high-anxiety situations
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Rely on the marketplace for late-night, teen, family, airport, or unfamiliar-city travel without feeling exposed or unsupported.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: The rider chooses the service in moments where trust matters, not only when convenience matters.

steps.step: Check whether this trip feels safe enough to request
steps.description: Interpret driver identity, trip-share controls, supervised-travel cues, and route confidence before committing.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Safety and Trust Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Makes safety tools visible before the rider needs them.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Trip Request and Intent Capture
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Preserves family, teen, and business account context in the booking flow.

media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Use ride sharing confidently in high-anxiety situations": Check whether this trip feels safe enough to request.

steps.step: Monitor the trip and stay connected
steps.description: Track route progress, share trip status, and act quickly if something looks wrong.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: ETA, Maps and Routing Intelligence
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shows route progress and expected trip-state changes.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Safety and Trust Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Handles live safety interventions, trip sharing, and anomaly detection.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider JTBD step 2: Monitor the trip and stay connected
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Use ride sharing confidently in high-anxiety situations": Monitor the trip and stay connected.

steps.step: Get resolution if the trip breaks trust
steps.description: Report incidents, billing issues, or pickup failures and get a credible response.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Rider Identity, Wallet and Payments
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports receipts, fare adjustment, and account-linked follow-up.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Safety and Trust Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Routes trust, fraud, and incident cases into operational response.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider JTBD step 3: Get resolution if the trip breaks trust
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Use ride sharing confidently in high-anxiety situations": Get resolution if the trip breaks trust.

media.alt: Enterprise infographic for the job "Use ride sharing confidently in high-anxiety situations" for customer "Urban Time-Sensitive Rider".

customerJourneyStories.name: Airport ride without pickup friction
customerJourneyStories.summary: A rider heading to the airport needs more than a cheap ride. The trip has to feel dependable enough that pickup, ETA, and luggage handling will not collapse under time pressure.

stages.narrative: A flight departure creates a hard deadline and almost no tolerance for pickup ambiguity or repeated cancellations.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider journey stage 1: Trigger
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 1 of "Airport ride without pickup friction": Trigger.

stages.narrative: The rider opens the app and compares immediate pickup, Reserve, and airport-aware products to decide how much certainty is worth paying for.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider journey stage 2: Discovery
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 2 of "Airport ride without pickup friction": Discovery.

stages.narrative: The rider judges upfront fare, ETA, pickup confidence, luggage fit, and whether a reserve or premium option will reduce risk.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider journey stage 3: Evaluation
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 3 of "Airport ride without pickup friction": Evaluation.

stages.narrative: The rider requests the trip and quickly tests whether the first match, pickup point, and route communication feel credible.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider journey stage 4: Trial
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "Airport ride without pickup friction": Trial.

stages.narrative: The rider watches the vehicle approach, adjusts to pickup guidance, tracks the route, and stays alert for anything that might jeopardize airport arrival.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider journey stage 5: Engagement
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 5 of "Airport ride without pickup friction": Engagement.

stages.narrative: If the trip feels predictable, well-guided, and fairly priced, the rider starts to trust the service for future high-stakes travel instead of using it only casually.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider journey stage 6: Retention
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 6 of "Airport ride without pickup friction": Retention.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider customer journey: Airport ride without pickup friction
media.alt: Enterprise journey storyboard for "Airport ride without pickup friction" for customer "Urban Time-Sensitive Rider".

customerJourneyStories.name: Late-night trip where trust matters more than speed
customerJourneyStories.summary: A rider leaving a night out uses the marketplace in a context where safety, pickup confidence, and support matter more than pure speed.

stages.narrative: The rider needs a safe trip home from a crowded or unfamiliar location late at night.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider journey stage 1: Trigger
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 1 of "Late-night trip where trust matters more than speed": Trigger.

stages.narrative: The rider checks available products, fare levels, wait times, and whether pickup conditions look chaotic.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider journey stage 2: Discovery
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 2 of "Late-night trip where trust matters more than speed": Discovery.

stages.narrative: The rider decides whether safety cues, driver identity, and trip-sharing tools make the ride feel trustworthy enough to request.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider journey stage 3: Evaluation
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 3 of "Late-night trip where trust matters more than speed": Evaluation.

stages.narrative: The rider requests the trip and immediately judges whether the assigned driver, pickup instructions, and route information reduce anxiety or increase it.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider journey stage 4: Trial
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "Late-night trip where trust matters more than speed": Trial.

stages.narrative: During the ride, the rider uses live trip tracking, sharing, and anomaly cues to stay confident that the trip is progressing normally.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider journey stage 5: Engagement
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 5 of "Late-night trip where trust matters more than speed": Engagement.

stages.narrative: When the rider feels protected and heard even if something goes wrong, the platform becomes the default choice in future trust-sensitive moments.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider journey stage 6: Retention
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 6 of "Late-night trip where trust matters more than speed": Retention.

media.title: Urban Time-Sensitive Rider customer journey: Late-night trip where trust matters more than speed
media.alt: Enterprise journey storyboard for "Late-night trip where trust matters more than speed" for customer "Urban Time-Sensitive Rider".

top.name: Reliable on-time trip completion
top.description: Share of requested trips that complete with acceptable pickup and arrival predictability.

children.name: ETA accuracy
children.description: Difference between promised and actual pickup or arrival timing.

children.name: Rider wait time
children.description: Time from request to pickup.

children.name: Avoidable cancellation rate
children.description: Trips lost because matching, pickup, or communication broke down.

children.name: Rematch recovery rate
children.description: Share of disrupted matches that still end in a completed trip.

children.name: Rider safety confidence
children.description: User-reported confidence that the trip feels controlled and supportable.

children.name: Safety feature usage quality
children.description: Trips where safety tools are available and usable when needed.

children.name: Issue recovery satisfaction
children.description: Rider satisfaction with how failed or risky trips are resolved.

children.name: Support resolution lead time
children.description: Time to first meaningful support action after a trip issue is reported.

top.name: Mobility gross bookings quality
top.description: Healthy ride volume and price realization without eroding trust or repeat use.

children.name: Request-to-trip conversion
children.description: Share of ride requests that become completed trips.

children.name: Repeat rider frequency
children.description: Average completed trips per active rider.

children.name: High-value trip mix
children.description: Share of trips coming from airport, reserve, business, and premium use cases.

children.name: Airport and reserve attach
children.description: Trips using airport or scheduled flows where certainty drives higher willingness to pay.

children.name: Marketplace take-rate quality
children.description: Economic capture that remains healthy without degrading rider trust or driver productivity.

children.name: Support cost per completed trip
children.description: Operational support cost required to keep rider trust intact.

productStrategy.vision: Make ride sharing feel dependable enough for everyday mobility and high-stakes travel moments, not just casual convenience.

1_year.focus: Pickup reliability, clearer pricing, and stronger airport or reserve experiences
milestones: Lower avoidable cancellations at peak hours
Airport pickup and reserve flows made more predictable
Safety toolkit surfaced more clearly in core trip moments

3_year.focus: More personalized product choice across budget, timing, and family needs
milestones: Shared and scheduled rides integrated into one clearer decision surface
Family and teen travel controls expanded where permitted
Airport and venue operations standardized across more cities

5_year.focus: Trusted mobility layer spanning on-demand, scheduled, shared, and future autonomous supply
milestones: Marketplace logic supports mixed human and autonomous supply where available
Scheduled and fixed-route products expand into more predictable commute use cases
Trip trust and identity controls become reusable across all mobility surfaces

customers.name: Flexible Driver Earner
customers.description: An independent driver using the platform to earn income with flexible hours. Success means staying productive, understanding earnings clearly, avoiding unsafe or unprofitable trips, and minimizing downtime caused by bad dispatch or support friction.
careAbout: Safety while driving and picking up riders
Fast issue resolution and account continuity
Fair treatment when edge cases happen
winsThem: Good matching at the right times and places
Heatmaps and incentives that are understandable and credible
Fast onboarding, verification, and document handling
Safety tools and protection from abuse or bad ratings
Fast access to earnings history and payout controls
theirFear: Spending too much time driving without paid trips
Unexpected account blocks or compliance friction
Unsafe riders, pickup zones, or incident handling

jobsToBeDone.name: Turn time online into predictable earnings
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Use the app to find worthwhile trips, stay in productive demand zones, and get paid clearly and on time.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher earnings per active hour with lower stress and less idle time.

steps.step: Get eligible and ready to drive
steps.description: Complete onboarding, background, vehicle, and document checks and stay compliant.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Driver Onboarding and Compliance
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Handles account setup, document checks, screening, and driver-status controls.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Safety and Trust Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Applies identity verification, fraud checks, and incident-prevention tooling.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner JTBD step 1: Get eligible and ready to drive
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Turn time online into predictable earnings": Get eligible and ready to drive.

steps.step: Find and accept productive work
steps.description: Go online, interpret demand signals, and receive trip offers worth taking.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Dispatch and Matching Engine
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Routes trip opportunities to the right driver based on location, quality, and rules.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Dynamic Pricing and Incentives
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Makes demand signals and earnings incentives legible enough to guide supply.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: ETA, Maps and Routing Intelligence
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Reduces wasted time on pickup navigation, airport queues, and handoff confusion.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner JTBD step 2: Find and accept productive work
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Turn time online into predictable earnings": Find and accept productive work.

steps.step: Track earnings and solve issues
steps.description: Understand what was earned, cash out, and resolve support or safety issues fast.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Driver Earnings and Incentive Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Breaks down fares, bonuses, and payout timing.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Safety and Trust Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports issue reporting, deactivation review, and on-trip safety escalation.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Measures earnings quality, acceptance behavior, and driver retention risk.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner JTBD step 3: Track earnings and solve issues
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Turn time online into predictable earnings": Track earnings and solve issues.

jobsToBeDone.name: Stay active on the platform without avoidable friction
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Keep driving over time without being pushed out by compliance confusion, payout mistrust, or poor support handling.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: The driver sees the platform as a credible long-term earnings channel rather than an unreliable side gig.

steps.step: Understand account standing and obligations
steps.description: Know what documents, behaviors, and market rules affect eligibility before a problem occurs.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Driver Onboarding and Compliance
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Makes status changes, requirements, and document deadlines explicit.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner JTBD step 1: Understand account standing and obligations
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Stay active on the platform without avoidable friction": Understand account standing and obligations.

steps.step: Get help when a trip or account issue appears
steps.description: Escalate unfair ratings, rider incidents, compliance disputes, or payout confusion into a credible workflow.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Safety and Trust Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Routes trust, incident, and case-management workflows.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Driver Earnings and Incentive Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides evidence and payout context when earnings are disputed.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner JTBD step 2: Get help when a trip or account issue appears
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Stay active on the platform without avoidable friction": Get help when a trip or account issue appears.

steps.step: Decide whether staying active is still worth it
steps.description: Compare effort, trust, and actual earnings quality to alternative ways of earning.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Highlights retention risks and productivity patterns.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Dynamic Pricing and Incentives
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shapes whether the platform still looks economically worthwhile.

customerJourneyStories.name: Peak-hour driving with less dead time
customerJourneyStories.summary: A driver opens the app during a limited, valuable time window and needs to convert that time into consistent, defensible earnings rather than confusing bursts of activity.

stages.narrative: The driver decides to go online during commute, nightlife, or airport peaks because those windows should offer the strongest earning potential.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner journey stage 1: Trigger
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 1 of "Peak-hour driving with less dead time": Trigger.

stages.narrative: The driver checks demand heatmaps, current incentives, airport conditions, and where supply already looks crowded.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner journey stage 2: Discovery
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 2 of "Peak-hour driving with less dead time": Discovery.

stages.narrative: The driver weighs whether the expected earnings justify fuel, time, congestion, and the risk of bad pickups or weak offers.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner journey stage 3: Evaluation
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 3 of "Peak-hour driving with less dead time": Evaluation.

stages.narrative: The driver goes online, accepts early offers, and tests whether actual trip quality matches what the marketplace signaled.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner journey stage 4: Trial
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "Peak-hour driving with less dead time": Trial.

stages.narrative: The driver chains rides, navigates pickups, monitors earnings, and adjusts where to wait based on what is actually happening on the road.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner journey stage 5: Engagement
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 5 of "Peak-hour driving with less dead time": Engagement.

stages.narrative: If active time turns into understandable earnings with manageable stress, the driver is more likely to keep returning to the platform.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner journey stage 6: Retention
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 6 of "Peak-hour driving with less dead time": Retention.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner customer journey: Peak-hour driving with less dead time

customerJourneyStories.name: Account problem that could trigger churn
customerJourneyStories.summary: A driver hits an account, compliance, or payout issue and judges whether the platform treats them fairly enough to keep driving.

stages.narrative: A missing document, payout discrepancy, safety complaint, or bad rider interaction puts the driver's account confidence at risk.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner journey stage 1: Trigger
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 1 of "Account problem that could trigger churn": Trigger.

stages.narrative: The driver tries to understand what changed, why it changed, and whether the problem is urgent or reversible.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner journey stage 2: Discovery
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 2 of "Account problem that could trigger churn": Discovery.

stages.narrative: The driver decides whether the in-app explanation and support path look credible enough to pursue rather than quitting.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner journey stage 3: Evaluation
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 3 of "Account problem that could trigger churn": Evaluation.

stages.narrative: The driver submits evidence, contacts support, or follows a remediation flow to test whether the platform responds coherently.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner journey stage 4: Trial
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "Account problem that could trigger churn": Trial.

stages.narrative: The driver tracks case progress, payout correction, or reinstatement steps while deciding whether to remain engaged.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner journey stage 5: Engagement
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 5 of "Account problem that could trigger churn": Engagement.

stages.narrative: Fast, fair, and transparent resolution makes continued driving feel viable; a weak response pushes the driver toward churn.

media.title: Flexible Driver Earner journey stage 6: Retention
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 6 of "Account problem that could trigger churn": Retention.

media.alt: Enterprise journey storyboard for "Account problem that could trigger churn" for customer "Flexible Driver Earner".

top.name: Driver earnings per active hour
top.description: Net earnings quality from time spent actively driving and serving trips.

children.name: Utilization rate
children.description: Share of online time spent in revenue-generating trip states.

children.name: Idle time
children.description: Time online without a paying trip.

children.name: Pickup efficiency
children.description: Time and mileage lost before trips start generating value.

children.name: Deadhead distance
children.description: Unpaid distance driven before rider pickup.

children.name: Earnings clarity score
children.description: Driver confidence that fare, bonus, and payout logic are understandable.

children.name: Payout dispute rate
children.description: Trips or periods triggering payout disputes or confusion.

children.name: Account standing confidence
children.description: Driver confidence that account status changes are fair and understandable.

children.name: Compliance interruption rate
children.description: Share of active drivers losing earning time to avoidable compliance interruptions.

top.name: Healthy driver supply availability
top.description: Enough active, high-quality supply to meet rider demand without destabilizing earnings.

children.name: Driver 7-day retention
children.description: Short-horizon retention for newly or recently active drivers.

children.name: Driver 30-day retention
children.description: Staying power after the first active period.

children.name: Offer acceptance quality
children.description: Driver participation on dispatched offers without excessive churn or low-quality matching.

children.name: Driver-side cancellation rate
children.description: Trips dropped because the dispatch opportunity did not remain worthwhile.

children.name: Peak-hour supply coverage
children.description: Share of rider demand windows where enough drivers are available.

children.name: Airport supply readiness
children.description: Coverage quality for airport and reserve-heavy contexts.

productStrategy.vision: Make the driver app feel like a trustworthy earnings operating system rather than a black box.

1_year.focus: Earnings transparency, better peak-hour dispatch, and lower support friction
milestones: Clearer trip and payout breakdowns
Better rematch behavior after rider cancellations
Faster support routing for safety and compliance events

3_year.focus: More supply segmentation and productivity controls for different driver types
milestones: Segment-specific flows for airport, reserve, teen, and shared products
Better supply planning by city and time window
Lower churn from avoidable deactivation and trust-process delays

5_year.focus: Human-supply management in a mixed marketplace with richer fleet and autonomous routing logic
milestones: Supply management works across individual drivers, fleets, and new vehicle types
Trust systems become more proactive and less punitive
Driver productivity tools remain credible as the network evolves

customers.name: Business Travel and Mobility Admin
customers.description: An administrator using Uber for Business to manage employee ground transportation, travel policy, spend controls, and visibility. Success means employees can travel easily while the company maintains policy compliance, spend oversight, and reporting.
careAbout: Support for airport and business-comfort use cases
Program adoption that does not depend on enforcement alone
winsThem: Central dashboard for policy and spend controls
Receipts and expense integration
Coverage across many cities and airports
Priority support and predictable billing
Sustainability and usage reporting
theirFear: Employees bypassing the program because the experience is weak
Poor controls over airport and premium trip spend
Fragmented reporting that makes finance reconciliation painful

jobsToBeDone.name: Run a compliant and convenient business mobility program
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Set policies, onboard employees, and give travelers a usable ride option that still meets company controls.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher program adoption with tighter spend and reporting discipline.

steps.step: Configure program rules and traveler access
steps.description: Set who can travel, when, how much can be spent, and which trip types are allowed.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Business and Fleet Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Handles policy, traveler groups, billing accounts, and admin settings.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Rider Identity, Wallet and Payments
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports business profiles, payment methods, and receipt routing.

media.title: Business Travel and Mobility Admin JTBD step 1: Configure program rules and traveler access
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Run a compliant and convenient business mobility program": Configure program rules and traveler access.

steps.step: Let employees request trips that stay inside policy
steps.description: Ensure the traveler experience remains familiar while preferred ride types and controls are applied.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Trip Request and Intent Capture
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports business-context request flows and traveler identity at request time.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Reserve, Share and Airport Orchestration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports airport, commute, and scheduled use cases common in corporate travel.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Dynamic Pricing and Incentives
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides pricing guardrails and product choice transparency.

media.title: Business Travel and Mobility Admin JTBD step 2: Let employees request trips that stay inside policy
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Run a compliant and convenient business mobility program": Let employees request trips that stay inside policy.

steps.step: Reconcile spend and improve policy
steps.description: Review usage, out-of-policy trips, receipts, and supplier performance to tune the program.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Business and Fleet Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides account reporting, policy exceptions, and billing management.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports cost, adoption, sustainability, and compliance reporting.

media.title: Business Travel and Mobility Admin JTBD step 3: Reconcile spend and improve policy
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Run a compliant and convenient business mobility program": Reconcile spend and improve policy.

media.alt: Enterprise infographic for the job "Run a compliant and convenient business mobility program" for customer "Business Travel and Mobility Admin".

jobsToBeDone.name: Prove the mobility program deserves broader rollout
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Translate traveler usage and spend control into evidence that finance, procurement, and leadership trust enough to expand the program.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: The program grows because it is easier to defend than unmanaged travel or reimbursement.

steps.step: Measure whether travelers actually adopt the program
steps.description: See whether employees choose the managed option instead of bypassing it.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Tracks traveler activation, active users, and category mix.

media.title: Business Travel and Mobility Admin JTBD step 1: Measure whether travelers actually adopt the program
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Prove the mobility program deserves broader rollout": Measure whether travelers actually adopt the program.

steps.step: Show policy and billing outcomes clearly
steps.description: Demonstrate whether spend controls, receipts, and exceptions are improving finance operations.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Business and Fleet Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Surfaces policy compliance and billing performance.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Rider Identity, Wallet and Payments
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides the underlying receipt and billing data needed for trust.

media.title: Business Travel and Mobility Admin JTBD step 2: Show policy and billing outcomes clearly
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Prove the mobility program deserves broader rollout": Show policy and billing outcomes clearly.

steps.step: Decide where to expand next
steps.description: Use usage, airport, commute, and premium-travel patterns to choose the next teams, cities, or policies to include.

customerJourneyStories.name: Travel program that employees actually use
customerJourneyStories.summary: A travel admin wants a managed program that improves control without forcing employees into a clumsy experience they will avoid.

stages.narrative: The company wants to centralize ground transport, cut unmanaged reimbursement, and reduce policy drift across teams.

media.title: Business Travel and Mobility Admin journey stage 1: Trigger
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 1 of "Travel program that employees actually use": Trigger.

stages.narrative: The admin evaluates coverage, airport strength, dashboard quality, receipt handling, and whether employees can stay in a familiar app experience.

media.title: Business Travel and Mobility Admin journey stage 2: Discovery
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 2 of "Travel program that employees actually use": Discovery.

stages.narrative: The admin tests if policy controls and billing rules are strong enough for finance while still leaving the traveler journey usable.

media.title: Business Travel and Mobility Admin journey stage 3: Evaluation
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 3 of "Travel program that employees actually use": Evaluation.

stages.narrative: A pilot launches with frequent travelers, airport-heavy teams, and a manageable set of spend controls.

media.title: Business Travel and Mobility Admin journey stage 4: Trial
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "Travel program that employees actually use": Trial.

stages.narrative: The admin monitors traveler adoption, out-of-policy trips, receipts, and the support burden created by the program.

media.title: Business Travel and Mobility Admin journey stage 5: Engagement
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 5 of "Travel program that employees actually use": Engagement.

stages.narrative: If the program becomes easier to justify than unmanaged travel, it expands into more teams and higher-value travel contexts.

media.title: Business Travel and Mobility Admin journey stage 6: Retention
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 6 of "Travel program that employees actually use": Retention.

media.title: Business Travel and Mobility Admin customer journey: Travel program that employees actually use

top.name: Business program adoption
top.description: Share of eligible travelers who actively use the managed mobility program.

children.name: Policy-compliant trip rate
children.description: Share of trips staying within configured travel policy.

children.name: Receipt capture quality
children.description: Trips with usable billing and receipt data.

children.name: Exception handling quality
children.description: How well the admin can understand and correct out-of-policy or broken billing cases.

children.name: Exception resolution time
children.description: Time to close a meaningful billing or policy exception.

children.name: Traveler activation rate
children.description: Eligible travelers who complete their first managed trip.

children.name: Managed traveler repeat rate
children.description: Travelers who continue using the managed profile beyond the first trip.

top.name: Business mobility revenue quality
top.description: Sustainable enterprise spend flowing through business programs with strong retention.

children.name: Net program growth
children.description: Growth in managed business accounts and spend within those accounts.

children.name: Airport and premium attach
children.description: Higher-value business trip mix from airport and premium travel use cases.

children.name: Billing trust score
children.description: Confidence from finance and admins that invoices, controls, and receipts are reliable.

children.name: Manual reconciliation load
children.description: Admin effort required to keep the program financially clean.

productStrategy.vision: Make Uber for Business the default ground-transport layer for companies that need convenience without losing control.

1_year.focus: Policy reliability, reporting, and airport-heavy business travel
milestones: Cleaner policy and billing controls
Better airport and reserve support for corporate travelers
Higher trust in finance-ready reporting

3_year.focus: Broader mobility-program management across commute, guests, and sustainability reporting
milestones: Travel, commute, and guest policies converge in one admin model
Deeper finance and HR integrations
More city-level reporting and cost controls

5_year.focus: Business mobility orchestration across mixed transport supply
milestones: Business policies span on-demand, scheduled, and fixed-route products
Program controls adapt by traveler type and city context
Enterprise reporting becomes a reason to standardize on the platform

customers.name: Fleet and Ground Operations Manager
customers.description: An operator managing multiple vehicles, drivers, or location-specific mobility operations such as airport supply, leased vehicles, or local fleet programs. Success means keeping vehicles utilized, drivers compliant, and operational exceptions manageable.
careAbout: Operational control at airports and high-demand zones
Low admin friction when adding or removing drivers
Clear proof of where the fleet is making or losing money
winsThem: Fleet dashboards and earnings reports
Fast supplier setup and driver assignment workflows
Clear airport and reserve operating logic
Lower downtime from compliance or support issues
Reliable APIs or portals for managing scale
theirFear: Vehicles sitting idle while demand exists
Driver paperwork and compliance problems blocking operations
Poor visibility into why earnings or utilization changed

jobsToBeDone.name: Keep a fleet productive and compliant on the marketplace
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Manage drivers, vehicles, and operating settings so the fleet captures demand without creating support or compliance drag.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Higher vehicle utilization with fewer avoidable compliance interruptions.

steps.step: Set up the supplier and vehicle estate
steps.description: Create the fleet account, assign drivers, and maintain vehicle eligibility.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Business and Fleet Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides supplier setup, vehicle assignment, and fleet reporting controls.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Driver Onboarding and Compliance
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps driver and vehicle compliance current.

media.title: Fleet and Ground Operations Manager JTBD step 1: Set up the supplier and vehicle estate
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Keep a fleet productive and compliant on the marketplace": Set up the supplier and vehicle estate.

steps.step: Run daily marketplace operations
steps.description: Manage availability, airport queues, reserve commitments, and exception handling while keeping vehicles productive.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Dispatch and Matching Engine
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Allocates requests across fleet drivers and time-sensitive jobs.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Reserve, Share and Airport Orchestration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports location-specific rules and scheduled-service commitments.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: ETA, Maps and Routing Intelligence
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Improves pickup execution in constrained zones such as airports and venues.

capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports fleet-level earnings and payout visibility.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Exposes utilization, trip quality, and operational trend reporting.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Safety and Trust Operations
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports incident resolution and trust governance at fleet scale.

jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Use utilization, earnings, compliance, and airport-specific operating signals to determine if more vehicles should be added or supply should be pulled back.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: The fleet grows only where marketplace economics and operational control justify it.

steps.step: Assess demand quality and operating constraints
steps.description: Understand how airport, reserve, venue, and city rules affect fleet productivity.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Measures where utilization and earnings are structurally strong or weak.

customerJourneyStories.name: Airport fleet with fewer operational surprises
customerJourneyStories.summary: A fleet operator wants airport and reserve-heavy demand without losing margin to queue confusion, compliance issues, or weak reporting.

stages.narrative: The operator expands into a city or airport where demand looks strong but operating rules are stricter and more brittle.

media.title: Fleet and Ground Operations Manager journey stage 1: Trigger
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 1 of "Airport fleet with fewer operational surprises": Trigger.

stages.narrative: The operator reviews supplier tooling, driver setup, airport logic, and whether the reporting is strong enough to run multiple vehicles.

media.title: Fleet and Ground Operations Manager journey stage 2: Discovery
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 2 of "Airport fleet with fewer operational surprises": Discovery.

stages.narrative: The operator tests whether utilization, reserve commitments, and queue rules create a controllable business rather than a chaotic one.

media.title: Fleet and Ground Operations Manager journey stage 3: Evaluation
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 3 of "Airport fleet with fewer operational surprises": Evaluation.

stages.narrative: A first set of vehicles and drivers goes live, exposing the real friction in onboarding, pickup lanes, and shift management.

media.title: Fleet and Ground Operations Manager journey stage 4: Trial
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "Airport fleet with fewer operational surprises": Trial.

stages.narrative: The operator monitors utilization, incidents, airport-specific constraints, and whether earnings visibility is good enough to steer the fleet day to day.

media.title: Fleet and Ground Operations Manager journey stage 5: Engagement
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 5 of "Airport fleet with fewer operational surprises": Engagement.

stages.narrative: When the fleet can predictably earn and stay compliant, the operator commits more vehicles and becomes more dependent on the platform.

media.title: Fleet and Ground Operations Manager journey stage 6: Retention
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 6 of "Airport fleet with fewer operational surprises": Retention.

media.alt: Enterprise journey storyboard for "Airport fleet with fewer operational surprises" for customer "Fleet and Ground Operations Manager".

top.name: Fleet vehicle utilization
top.description: Share of fleet vehicle time that converts into productive trip service.

children.name: Compliance-ready vehicle share
children.description: Vehicles and drivers eligible to accept trips without administrative blockers.

children.name: Reserve fulfillment rate
children.description: Scheduled or reserved trips fulfilled as planned.

children.name: Airport operating quality
children.description: How well the fleet handles queue, pickup, and constrained-location rules.

children.name: Queue delay burden
children.description: Time lost to airport or venue holding patterns before productive work begins.

children.name: Fleet earnings visibility
children.description: Ability to understand and sustain fleet economics over time.

children.name: Unexplained earnings variance
children.description: Share of earnings changes the operator cannot confidently explain.

top.name: Fleet partner growth quality
top.description: Healthy expansion of fleet-driven supply without destabilizing quality or unit economics.

children.name: Fleet retention
children.description: Retention of active fleet partners in priority markets.

children.name: Expansion willingness
children.description: Fleet operators ready to add more vehicles in-market.

children.name: Priority-window fleet coverage
children.description: Fleet contribution in airport, reserve, and hard-to-cover demand windows.

children.name: Supply quality in constrained zones
children.description: How well fleet supply performs in high-friction locations.

productStrategy.vision: Give fleet operators enough control and visibility to scale supply on the platform without having to build their own marketplace stack.

1_year.focus: Supplier setup, earnings visibility, and airport or reserve operations

revenueStreams: Marketplace take rate on fleet supply

milestones: Cleaner fleet setup and driver assignment
Better earnings and utilization reporting
Lower airport and reserve exception handling

3_year.focus: More partner-grade tooling and integration surfaces
milestones: Higher-fidelity supplier portal and APIs
Segmented controls for fleet types and cities
Better shared accountability on safety and compliance

5_year.focus: Partner supply management across mixed vehicle and operating models
milestones: Fleet controls support more vehicle and supply models
Partner tooling becomes a reason to consolidate supply on the marketplace
Operational reporting spans human and future autonomous partner supply where relevant
